skill.name: react-useeffect
skill.description: Guidance for deciding when React useEffect is necessary and when render logic, event handlers, useMemo, key props, or useSyncExternalStore are better choices.
skill.summary: Review React useEffect usage and replace unnecessary Effects with simpler React patterns.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, storage, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found markdown documentation and fenced React examples rather than executable skill behavior. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, or malicious intent was found; residual risk is low because examples discuss fetch, local storage, and auth-token handling.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated external command findings are inline code ticks and fenced TypeScript examples in documentation. They do not invoke a shell or define executable skill scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are markdown tables or fenced React examples. No executable Ruby, shell command, or script entry point is present in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Network References Are Educational Examples
low_findings.description: The network findings point to prose about network requests, a generic fetch example, and official React documentation links. No skill code performs outbound requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch call appears inside a React custom hook example, and the URLs are source references to react.dev. There is no evidence of hidden telemetry or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage And Auth Mentions Are Guidance
low_findings.description: Browser storage and auth-token references appear in an anti-pattern about app initialization. The text warns that React Strict Mode can expose missing cleanup or unsafe repeated initialization.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The storage and token names are illustrative placeholder functions in markdown examples. They are not implemented functions and no sensitive data is read by the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Matching Noise
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur on documentation lines about React Effects, reference files, and guide structure. No cryptographic API or hashing implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose and filenames, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic operations. No evidence of cryptographic behavior was found.

content.user_title: Review React Effects Before You Write Them
content.value_statement: React developers often use useEffect for work React can handle during render or in events. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code choose simpler hook patterns and safer cleanup.
actual_capabilities: Identifies cases where derived values should be calculated during render.
Recommends useMemo for expensive calculations with stable dependencies.
Explains when key props should reset component state.
Moves event-specific logic from Effects into event handlers.
Reviews data fetching Effects for cleanup and race-condition handling.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not automated static analysis.
It does not replace React framework data loading features.
It cannot verify runtime behavior without the application code.
It focuses on useEffect patterns, not all React architecture decisions.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Check whether new Effects are necessary and whether cleanup is missing.

use_cases.title: Refactor Hook Logic
use_cases.description: Replace derived state, Effect chains, and event side effects with simpler React patterns.

use_cases.title: Teach Hook Decisions
use_cases.description: Explain why a team should use render logic, event handlers, or subscriptions instead of Effects.

prompt_templates.title: Check If I Need an Effect
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component and tell me whether each useEffect is necessary. Suggest a simpler React pattern when possible.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Derived State
prompt_templates.prompt: Find any state that is derived from props or other state. Rewrite the approach using render-time calculation or useMemo.

prompt_templates.title: Review Data Fetching Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this data fetching Effect for race conditions, stale responses, and missing cleanup. Recommend a safer pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Effect Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these components for unnecessary Effects, chained updates, subscription mistakes, and parent-child synchronization issues.

output_examples.input: A form stores fullName in state and updates it from firstName and lastName inside an Effect.
output_examples.output: The Effect is unnecessary. Calculate the full name during render so the UI avoids an extra stale render.

output_examples.input: A search component fetches results when the query changes but does not ignore stale responses.
output_examples.output: Add cleanup for stale requests or use a framework data loader. This prevents older responses from replacing newer results.

output_examples.input: A product page shows a notification from an Effect after cart state changes.
output_examples.output: Move the notification into the buy click handler. The event handler knows the exact user action that caused the message.

best_practices: Start by asking whether an external system is involved.
Calculate values during render unless the calculation is expensive.
Return cleanup from Effects that subscribe, fetch, or set timers.
anti_patterns: Do not store values that can be derived from current props or state.
Do not use Effects to handle a specific button click or form submit.
Do not chain multiple Effects to calculate one state transition.

faq.question: When should I use useEffect?
faq.answer: Use it to synchronize with external systems, such as browser APIs, subscriptions, widgets, analytics, or data fetching.

faq.question: When should I avoid useEffect?
faq.answer: Avoid it for derived values, event-specific logic, state resets, and updates that can happen during render.

faq.question: Does this skill write React code?
faq.answer: It guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to review and rewrite useEffect patterns when you provide code.

faq.question: Can it help with race conditions?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights stale async responses and suggests cleanup flags, AbortController, or framework data loading.

faq.question: Does it replace React Query or SWR?
faq.answer: No. It often recommends framework or library data fetching when manual Effects add unnecessary complexity.

faq.question: Is it only for senior React developers?
faq.answer: No. It includes beginner decision rules and deeper review prompts for experienced teams.
